text: Is this item actually mine to make decisions about?
Would I notice if this were gone?
Is this something I am keeping out of guilt or obligation?
Does this item align with my future life?
If I died tomorrow, would anyone actually want to deal with this?
Would I be embarrassed for someone to find this?
Does this item ever come to mind when I'm not looking directly at it?
Would I choose this item again today, based on my current values?
Does this item feel aligned with the life I'm trying to create?
Is this item meaningful because of a memory or relationship it represents?
Does this item help me feel safe?
Do I have more than one item that represents this same memory or feeling?
Could I preserve the meaning of this item in a way that doesn't require keeping the object?
Would I be able to keep just one item from the category this belongs to instead of all of them?
Does the idea of letting this go bring up fear, anxiety, or emotional discomfort for me?
If this item disappeared, would anything important in my life actually change?
Is this item aligned with the version of myself I'm growing into?
Does this item contribute to my sense of freedom?
If I had to pay a baggage fee for this, would I keep it?
Does this item spark joy?
Does this item belong in the next chapter of my life?
Will this have a meaningful place in my life if I keep it?
Am I keeping this because of the time or effort I've already invested in it?
Am I keeping this because I'm avoiding the feelings that come with letting it go?
Is this item tied to a version of myself I'm no longer living as (or wish I were)?
Would it cost less than a typical takeout meal to replace this item if I needed it again?
If I let this go and later regret it, would it be easy to find and replace?
Is this valuable enough to justify the time and effort it would take me to sell it?
Would I feel okay donating this instead of trying to sell it?
Would I still keep this if I ignored what I originally paid for it?
Is the ongoing cost (maintenance, supplies, storage) of this item worth what I get from it?
Is this worth at least $10-$20 and simple enough to sell that it wouldn't become a burden?
If I listed this today, is it realistic that someone would actually buy it?
Is this item primarily clothing or an accessory?
Is this item primarily paperwork, documents, or mail?
Is this item primarily a tool, appliance, or functional equipment?
Is this item primarily décor or a decorative object?
Is this item primarily a hobby, craft, or project supply?
Is this item primarily a sentimental object or keepsake?
Is this item primarily a consumable (food, product, supply that gets used up)?
Is this item primarily electronics or something that stores data?
Is this item part of a set or collection that only makes sense together?
Is this item seasonal?
Is this a living thing (plant, pet, other organism etc.) that depends on me for care?
If I donate this item, will it realistically be used or wanted?
Is this an official document I may be legally required to keep (ID, tax, medical, legal)?
Does this item contain sensitive personal information that should be shredded or wiped before disposal?
Is this item considered hazardous waste (batteries, chemicals, electronics, sharps, etc.)?
Is this an item that donation centers typically do not accept (e.g., certain bedding, broken items)?
Would throwing this in regular trash be unsafe, illegal, or environmentally harmful?
If I can't or don't want to keep caring for this, can I responsibly rehome or transfer it?
Do I avoid using this because it's inconvenient, fiddly, or high-effort?
Is this seasonal item still relevant to my current lifestyle?
Does this item serve a meaningful purpose in my life right now?
Do I want this?
Do I need this?
Do I use this on a regular or predictable basis?
Does this improve my life?
Would I buy or collect this again today?
Do I have more than one of this item?
Does this item perform more than one job?
Has this item been used or added value to my life in the past year?
Is this item genuinely useful in my life?
Do I have a specific, realistic use for this item in the next 3 months?
Do I have a specific, realistic use for this item in the next 12 months?
Does this item still work exactly as intended?
Does this item have all the parts or accessories it needs to function?
Do I already own something else that does the same job as well or better?
When I need the function this item provides, is this the thing I actually reach for?
Is this item easy for me to use without extra setup, tools, or effort?
Is this item comfortable or pleasant to use (not painful, awkward, or frustrating)?
Do I already have a digital version of this item that serves the same purpose?
Will I be able to use this product before its expiration/end-of-life date?
Is this item safe, sanitary, and in good enough condition to keep?
Is this item made to last?
Does the item expire or have an end-of-life?
Is this item in a condition where I actually want to use it?
Is this item visibly dirty, moldy, stained, or otherwise unhygienic?
Is this item expired, spoiled, or past its safe use date?
Is this item damaged in a way that makes it unsafe to use?
Would I feel comfortable giving this item to someone I care about in its current condition?
Is this item a potential hazard for pets, kids, or people in my home?
Does this item have sharp edges, frayed cords, or other safety risks?
Is this item so worn out that I avoid using it even when it's available?
Is this living thing thriving in my care, or just surviving?
Does the value of this item justify the real estate it occupies?
Is this worth packing, moving, and unpacking?
Can this be digitized, downsized, or simplified?
Do I have a clear, specific place to store this item in my [new] home?
Will this item fit comfortably in that space without crowding or overflow?
Is this item small or light enough that moving it feels reasonable?
If I keep this, will it force something else I value to lose space?
Does this item tend to migrate into clutter piles instead of staying put?
Is this item awkwardly shaped or bulky in a way that makes storage difficult?
Is this item suited to the climate, lifestyle, or space of my [new] home?
Does this item require special storage (humidity, temperature, protection) that I cannot realistically maintain?
Can I or someone else responsibly extend its life?
Can the item be repaired, repurposed, or upcycled instead of discarded?
Is this item worth the time, space or energy it demands of me?
Would I carry this item on my back for 3 miles in the rain?
Would I grab this item if my house was burning down?
Does this item require regular maintenance, cleaning, or upkeep to stay usable?
Am I realistically willing to do that maintenance going forward?
Is this item part of a project I've been postponing for more than 6 months?
If I keep this, will it add to my mental to-do list in a way that feels heavy?
Does this item create more chores (cleaning around it, moving it, organizing it) than it saves?
Have I already tried to “deal with” this item multiple times and put it back each time?
If I keep this, will I need to buy additional things (parts, containers, tools) just to support it?
Does this item require skills, habits, or hobbies I no longer maintain?
Does this item leave me feeling heavier, worse, or more drained than before I interacted with it?
Am I realistically willing and able to care for this?
